summary: ICIS: Vietnam PP yarn/inj +30 to 990-1,020 USD/ton CFR last week
Crude strength lifted China PP spot; SE Asia/China homo +~30 USD
South commercial sellers push PP homo up; north holds cautiously
paras: According to ICIS pricing published on the last trading day of last week, 17/7, PP yarn/injection prices in Vietnam rose USD 30/ton versus a week earlier, to USD 990-1,020/ton/CFR. More broadly across the region, PP homo prices in the Southeast Asian and Chinese markets both rose about USD 30/ton; PP block copo went from flat to up USD 20-30/ton; conversely, PP random copo prices fell about USD 10-30/ton.

Pricing from other international organizations also showed PP prices rebounding last week. The driver was crude oil rising sharply as tensions between the US and Iran escalated. Some suppliers even stopped offering at the end of the week to watch whether the crude oil rally would continue. Some customers returned to the market to buy for immediate production needs, but they did not want to stockpile much, believing the price rally would quickly stall once the situation in the Middle East changes.

Although cargo supply from Korea increased, offer prices remained relatively competitive versus other suppliers; however, competition may become fiercer as a large producer in Malaysia now has both resin grades available – for yarn/weaving and for injection. Because import demand in Southeast Asia remains fairly quiet, some suppliers and traders are targeting other markets, such as China, to achieve better net margins. Prices in China are currently high, reopening arbitrage opportunities from Southeast Asia to China and leading some Vietnamese cargoes to be sold into this market.

Crude oil holding high supported the broad price rise across China's basic chemicals market, pushing PP spot prices there up sharply week-on-week. However, by mid-week the rise in China's PP spot prices began to stall.

Today, 20/7, on the first trading day of the new week, market sentiment is very cautious. In the north, most sellers temporarily held last weekend's offers or had not yet quoted new prices, gauging more of the market situation before deciding on price adjustments.

In the south, however, many commercial sellers continued to adjust offer prices up sharply, especially PP homo. Whereas at the end of last week PP yarn/injection offer and transaction prices in the south were common at VND 31,000-32,700/kg incl. VAT depending on origin and grade, today the offer level rose to VND 32,500-34,000/kg incl. VAT. Of this, PP yarn/injection of domestic origin from Hyosung Vina and BSR, or Hanwha, Daelim and S-Oil Korea cargoes, was offered at about VND 32,500-33,000/kg incl. VAT, up sharply VND 1,500-2,000/kg versus the end of last week. PP copo, Film and coating items fluctuated less than PP, mostly holding flat or edging up slightly.

Nonetheless, the aforementioned commercial price increase may be a move to test market reaction. In fact, in the north most commercial sellers offered PP yarn 114G and PP injection 105G of NSRP at VND 32,000-32,500/kg incl. VAT for immediate delivery, but buyers were still not ready to buy at this level. Many buyers chose to sign for cargoes delivered in early August, with offers about VND 500/kg lower than for immediate delivery.

"In this mid-July rally, many commercial sellers consumed more goods and reduced inventory significantly. Many large distributors actively pushed exports instead of selling domestically. This left some items and grades slightly scarce and prices pushed high. However, supply is expected to be more stable in early August, and the high price level will be hard to sustain,".

While the PP homo rally temporarily paused at the end of the week, PP copo offers from some distributors today recorded an upward adjustment again (PP copo prices fell early this week, then eased from mid-week). For example, in the south, PP copo offers from Hyosung Vina rose again to VND 34,500-35,000/kg incl. VAT, after falling sharply to VND 34,000/kg incl. VAT early in the week. As of today, Hyosung Vina's plant has reportedly temporarily stopped offering.

For PP coating, M9600 of Lotte Korea was offered in the south at VND 42,000-43,000/kg incl. VAT. In the north, few sellers hold M9600 inventory for immediate delivery, creating a slightly scarce situation.

summary: ICIS: Vietnam LDPE Film +60 to 1,130-1,180 USD/ton CFR last week
ZPC China LLDPE 1,245-1,265; LDPE 1,445; HDPE 1,225 USD/ton CIF
Domestic HDPE 35,000-37,000 VND/kg; buyers cautious at new highs
paras: According to ICIS pricing published on the last trading day of last week, 17/7, LDPE Film prices in Vietnam rose sharply by USD 60/ton last week to USD 1,130-1,180/ton/CFR; LLDPE Film rose USD 10-20/ton to USD 920-980/ton/CFR; HDPE Film rose USD 30-40/ton to USD 970-1,040/ton/CFR; HDPE injection also rose USD 30-40/ton to USD 940-1,040/ton/CFR; and HDPE blow rose USD 70/ton to USD 970-1,070/ton/CFR... More broadly across the region, PE prices in Southeast Asia and China both rose last week.

The Asian PE market gained last week, boosting active offering activity for cargoes originating from the Middle East and the Americas. Because shipping schedules from the Middle East remain uncertain, buyers are giving preference to US-origin cargoes that have specific expected port-arrival timing. China's PE export prices also rose after it recorded an increase in purchase inquiries from abroad.

In the Southeast Asian PE import market, most suppliers either raised offers or withdrew from the market, stoking concerns about supply shortages. However, this price increase remains uncertain. Many buyers say they are not rushing to place orders – markedly different from the period when the new conflict erupted in Iran in early March – especially as supply appears still abundant. HDPE prices remain higher than LLDPE due to tighter HDPE supply.

Today, 20/7, on the first trading day of the new week, PE import offer prices from China to Vietnam continued to rise sharply versus the end of last week. Notably, the distributor of ZPC China offered LLDPE Film/injection to Vietnam at USD 1,245-1,265/ton/CIF, LLDPE roto offered at 1,325; LDPE Film offered at USD 1,445/ton/CIF; HDPE offered at USD 1,225/ton/CIF, HDPE blow offered at 1,275/ton/CIF... These offer prices rose very sharply, given that last week LLDPE Film offers from Petrochina China were at USD 1,050-1,060/ton/CIF, with some distributors offering Chinese cargoes at USD 1,160-1,180/ton/CIF...

Domestically, after cautiously monitoring the market on the morning of the first trading day, by this afternoon the market recorded many sellers officially raising prices sharply, especially for LLDPE. According to In the north some sellers have raised LLDPE Film of Sabic and Qamar origin to VND 35,500-36,000/kg incl. VAT; LLDPE Film of Chinese, Korean and Southeast Asian origin rose to VND 33,500-35,000/kg incl. VAT. This new offer level for LLDPE is up sharply by VND 2,000-3,000/kg versus the end of last week.

Meanwhile, HDPE Film/yarn/blow/injection offer prices ranged commonly at VND 35,000-37,000/kg incl. VAT, from flat to up about VND 500-1,000/kg versus the end of last week. LDPE offer levels also went from flat to slightly up VND 500-1,000/kg versus the end of last week, depending on the item and supplier.
According to sources, traders pushed offer prices high, but buyers remained cautious. Not many buyers have signed for cargoes at the new price level.

summary: Sinopec S1000/K67 offered 695-700 CIF Hai Phong / 700-705 HCMC
SCG Thailand SP660/SG660 firm-sold 710-720 USD/ton CIF (Aug)
Buyers bid ~20 USD below offers; sellers hold, refuse cuts
paras: Today, 20/7, a large distributor offered imported PVC S1000/K67 of Sinopec China origin to Vietnam at USD 695-700/ton/CIF Hai Phong and USD 700-705/ton/CIF HCMC, depending on payment terms. Immediate delivery in July. Per sources, most buyers bid import prices about USD 20/ton lower than the above offer schedule.

At the end of last week, the distributor of SCG Thailand set a firm selling price for the August 2026 PVC lot for our country's buyers, with PVC SP660/SG660 firm-sold at USD 710-720/ton/CIF. The number of buyers who firm-booked imports was not large, as this level was assessed as still fairly high versus buyers' expectations.

With PVC (oil-based) offers for the August lot from some large distributors at USD 690-720/ton/CIF as noted above, domestic PVC sellers also held prices more firmly and did not accept further reductions.

Notably, in the middle of last week, domestic producer AGC Vina (ACVN) quoted separately for some large customers seeking early prices for the August 2026 PVC lot, with PVC PM66R at only VND 18,800/kg excl. VAT (adding the full 8% VAT brings it to about VND 20,300/kg). However, the plant has temporarily stopped offering to consider official pricing for the August lot, expected in the middle to end of this week.

In the commercial market, today also sporadically recorded some sellers edging PVC prices up slightly, including both oil-based and coal-based PVC. Some sellers explained that coal-based PVC offers edged up VND 100-200/kg versus the end of last week due to rising freight rates from China.

On the buyer side, however, most still purchase cautiously and are not ready to firm-book imports at higher prices. Currently, PVC offer and transaction prices are common at VND 20,000-22,000/kg incl. VAT.
